name: Requena, Chera, and Sierra de Chiva Loop
name_original: Gravel ride from Requena to Chera and sierra de Chiva
description: This difficult 58.8-mile (94.6 km) gravel ride from Requena to Chera and Sierra de Chiva offers challenging climbs and scenic views.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Requena offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural landscapes. The region features a mix of unpaved roads and tracks, often passing through vineyards and historical areas. Elevations vary, with some routes including significant climbs and descents. Best gravel bike trails around Requena The most popular gravel bike trail is Requena, Chera, and Sierra de Chiva Loop, a 58.8 miles (94.6 km) trail that takes 11 hours to complete. This route traverses varied terrain including the Sierra de Chiva. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Villargordo del Cabriel – Universal Monument to the Grape Harvest loop from Requena, a difficult 72.0 miles (115.9 km) path. This route explores agricultural landscapes and river valleys. Local gravel bikers also love the Castle and walls of Requena – Requena Fortress loop from Requena, a 70.0 miles (112.7 km) trail leading through historical sites and elevated viewpoints, often completed in about 9 hours 55 minutes. Gravel biking around Requena is defined by hilly terrain, river valleys, and agricultural areas. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Requena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 160 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Requena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of gravel biking trails in Requena?
A: Gravel biking in Requena offers a range of challenges. The region features routes suitable for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with 11 moderate and 18 difficult tours available. Expect hilly terrain, river valleys, and agricultural areas, often including significant climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any long-distance gravel routes available in Requena?
A: Yes, Requena offers several long-distance gravel routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cabriel – Way of the Cross of Caravaca loop from Villatoya&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 56.3 km (35 miles) route that explores the natural beauty of the area. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle Slope – Requena Fortress loop from Requena&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 65.2 km (40.5 miles) and offers historical insights.

Q: Where can I find parking for gravel biking trails in Requena?
A: Many gravel biking routes in Requena start directly from the town of Requena or nearby villages like Villatoya. You can typically find public parking facilities within these towns, often near the main squares or designated parking areas, making it convenient to access the trailheads.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Requena?
A: The Requena region experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for gravel biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons also showcase the vineyards and natural landscapes at their best.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly gravel bike trails in Requena?
A: While many routes in Requena are classified as moderate to difficult due to the hilly terrain and varied landscapes, there are options that can be adapted for less experienced riders. It's recommended to check the elevation profiles and distances of routes on komoot to find one that matches your comfort level. Currently, the guide lists tours primarily in the moderate and difficult categories.

Q: What can I see along the gravel bike trails in Requena?
A: The gravel bike trails in Requena pass through diverse landscapes including vineyards, river valleys, and historical areas. You can encounter impressive natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5367776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Buitre&lt;/a&gt; canyon. Some routes also lead past historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and walls of Requena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike trails in Requena?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike routes in Requena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1668161217/requena-chera-and-sierra-de-chiva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena, Chera, and Sierra de Chiva Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villargordo del Cabriel – Universal Monument to the Grape Harvest loop from Requena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy the most about gravel biking in Requena?
A: The gravel biking routes in Requena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which includes rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural landscapes, as well as the opportunity to explore historical areas and vineyards.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on Requena gravel bike trails?
A: Yes, many routes offer elevated viewpoints due to the region's hilly nature. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and walls of Requena – Requena Fortress loop from Requena&lt;/a&gt; includes sections with historical sites and elevated vistas, providing panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes and vineyards.

Q: How many gravel bike trails are there in Requena?
A: There are over 25 gravel bike trails available in the Requena region, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the diverse terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, some routes may skirt or pass through areas of natural beauty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1668161217/requena-chera-and-sierra-de-chiva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena, Chera, and Sierra de Chiva Loop&lt;/a&gt; traverses varied terrain including the Sierra de Chiva, which is known for its natural landscapes. You might also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1055781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sot de Chera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; near some routes.
tips: Main coast and gateway to Barri de la Vil·la, declared a national historical-artistic complex in 1966.
Near Siete Aguas are the remains of this undated aqueduct. Due to the type of construction, it could be a Roman work.

This climb is a classic section for local cyclists. Many of the usual routes in the area pass through here, as it is a good climb with beautiful views that leads to the La Loma aerodrome.
From there, and after a brief rest, the ascent continues towards the natural area of Sierra de Chiva.

The Buseo reservoir dams the Reatillo river. It was built to irrigate the garden crops, although now its use is basically recreational.
There is a camping area where, with prior permission, you can spend the night. It also has picnic areas, a fountain, toilets and showers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this gravel ride?
faqs.answer: This gravel ride is classified as difficult, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for significant climbing, and there are sections where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mixed surface. You'll encounter approximately 62.8 km of unpaved tracks, 17.9 km of asphalt, and 13.3 km of paved sections. Overall, it consists of well-maintained paths (67.0 km), roads (24.2 km), and streets (3.47 km).

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass several notable landmarks and scenic spots. These include the historic Castle Slope, the ancient Aqueduct ruins near Siete Aguas, and the challenging Climb to the heliport, which offers beautiful views. You'll also ride past the scenic Buseo Reservoir.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this gravel ride?
faqs.answer: The ride typically starts in Requena. You can look for parking options near the Requena train station or other public parking areas within the town to begin your adventure.

faqs.question: Is this gravel ride dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in this region, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through natural parks like the Parque Natural de Chera-Sot de Chera and Paraje Natural Municipal Sierra de Chiva, and near any livestock. Given the route's difficulty and length, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness level.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required for cycling this route, even though it passes through the Parque Natural de Chera-Sot de Chera and Paraje Natural Municipal Sierra de Chiva. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding nature conservation and private property.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to do this gravel ride?
faqs.answer: The autumn months are particularly recommended for this ride, especially if you want to experience the 'Tierra Bobal' region with its vineyards in their vibrant colors. Spring is also a great option with pleasant temperatures. Summers can be very hot, so an early start is advisable if riding during that season.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of 'De Juan Vich a Casas del Río', 'Camí Natural Turia - Cabriel', and the 'Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Valencia', among others. It also touches upon various segments around the 'Embalse de Buseo'.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: The ride starts in Requena and passes through Chera, both of which offer amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in these towns. It's always a good idea to plan your stops and carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer, more remote sections between towns.

faqs.question: What are the regulations or special considerations when cycling through the Parque Natural de Chera-Sot de Chera and Paraje Natural Municipal Sierra de Chiva?
faqs.answer: When cycling through these natural parks, it's crucial to stay on marked trails, respect wildlife, and avoid disturbing the natural environment. Take all your rubbish with you, and be mindful of fire restrictions, especially during dry seasons. While no specific cycling permits are usually needed, always check for any temporary local advisories or regulations.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach the starting point in Requena?
faqs.answer: Yes, Requena is accessible by public transport. There is a train station in Requena, which can be a convenient option for cyclists looking to reach the starting point of the gravel ride. Check local train schedules for connections from major cities like Valencia.
